title: The Briefing
tagline: AI intelligence for Gauntlet AI — April 5, 2026

items.headline: 6 Wellfound Signals to Know Before Joining a Startup
items.summary: Wellfound's latest guide breaks down how job seekers can vet startups before applying: dig into founder history under the 'People' tab, scan press mentions for funding/IPO/acquisition signals, check team pages to put faces to names, review exact funding stage and company maturity, scrutinize investor rosters via the 'Funding' tab, and use the 'Culture' tab for perks, photos, and video. The framework also links to Wellfound's startup-vs-corporate guide for candidates deciding which environment suits them.

items.headline: Startup vs. Corporate: Wellfound Lays Out the Trade-offs
items.summary: Wellfound published a companion blog post helping candidates decide between early-to-mid-stage startup life and corporate environments. Key factors: risk tolerance, preference for ownership vs. structure, and how much a candidate values speed of iteration over stability. The post is aimed at the surge of talent now evaluating both AI startups and enterprise AI roles.

items.headline: Wellfound Job Market: AI Startup Roles Still Flowing
items.summary: Wellfound's jobs board continues to list thousands of open roles at AI-native startups, from seed to Series C. With Sora shutting down and talent reshuffling across the industry, now is a good window for teams recruiting AI engineers — candidates with Gauntlet-caliber skills are actively exploring options.
